records.ety: [OF. ensevre, OF. & F. ensuivre, fr. L. insequi; in + sequi to pursue. See Sue.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ensued (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Ensuing.]

defs: To follow; to pursue; to follow and overtake.
quotes: To ensue his example in doing the like mischief.

defs: To follow or come afterward; to follow as a consequence or in chronological succession; to result; as, an ensuing conclusion or effect; the year ensuing was a cold one.
quotes: So spoke the Dame, but no applause ensued.
Damage to the mind or the body, or to both, ensues, unless the exciting cause be presently removed.
